message:
fix(cohere,tools): preserve assistant text alongside tool_calls and reorder process tree collection

cohere provider (src/providers/cohere.rs):
- Removes the tool_calls.is_empty() guard from the assistant message
  content field assignment in build_cohere_messages(). Previously, when
  an assistant message contained both text AND tool_calls, the text was
  silently dropped (content set to None). This caused the Cohere API to
  lose the assistant's reasoning/explanation that preceded tool
  invocations, which could degrade conversation coherence in multi-turn
  tool-use flows. Now, assistant text is preserved whenever it's
  non-empty, regardless of whether tool_calls are also present.
- Adds test_build_request_assistant_text_preserved_alongside_tool_calls
  integration test that constructs an assistant message with both a text
  block ("Let me read that file.") and a tool call, then verifies the
  serialized request preserves the text in the content field alongside
  the tool_calls array.

process management (src/tools.rs):
- Reorders kill_process_tree_with() to collect the full process tree
  (sysinfo refresh, children map construction, BFS traversal) BEFORE
  sending the PGID kill signal. Previously, the PGID kill was sent
  first, which could cause child processes to exit before sysinfo had
  a chance to enumerate them, resulting in an incomplete tree and
  orphaned grandchildren surviving the kill. By capturing the tree
  snapshot first, all descendants are reliably identified even if some
  exit during the subsequent PGID kill.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
